what: Tools that make AquaCropv7 files handling easier
title: Tools that make AquaCropv7 files handling easier
text: aquacroptools set tools specifically developed work AquaCrop v7.0 (August 2022) files. AquaCrop crop-water productivity model developed Food Agriculture Organization (FAO) United Nations. used simulate evaluate response various crops water availability provides insights crop water requirements, yield potential, water productivity. aquacroptools designed make Aquacrop v7.0 files handling much easier allow users programmatically : Generate AquaCrop v7.0 files. Read,manipulate Aquacrop v7.0 files.

title: Tools that make AquaCropv7 files handling easier
text: can install development version aquacroptools GitHub :
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("oousmane/aquacroptools")

title: Tools that make AquaCropv7 files handling easier

code: library(aquacroptools) library(fs) ## basic example code data("weather") # load example dataset  head(weather) # check the data sets structure. The column names should be as is.  if(!dir_ls("climate/")) dir_create("climate/") # output directories  write_plu(path = "climate/") # write .PLU file write_tnx(path = "climate/") # write .Tnx file write_et0(path = "climate/") # write .ETo file write_cli(   path = "climate/", #path were we have <<station>>.* (PLU,ETo, Tnx)   stn ="wakanda", # name of the station <<station>>.* (PLU,ETo, Tnx)   scenario ="hist" # climate data scenario : hist, rcpxx or sspxxx,   check_files = TRUE   )

title: Build a Crop Parameter Set — build_crop_parameters
text: crop_cycle Integer. Total crop cycle length days. Default = 90 flowering_date Integer. Days sowing flowering. Default = 35 plant_spacing Numeric. Distance plants within rows (m). Default = 0.5 row_spacing Numeric. Distance rows (m). Default = 0.8 var_28 Numeric. Minimum canopy cover (%) yield formation stops. Default = 40 var_38 Numeric. Soil fertility stress coefficient (0-1). Default = 1 (stress) var_08 Numeric. Base temperature (deg C) growth occurs. Default = 8.0 var_09 Numeric. Upper temperature (deg C) growth optimal. Default = 30

what: Get Crop Stages Lengths and Calendar — calculate_crop_stages
title: Get Crop Stages Lengths and Calendar — calculate_crop_stages
text: Compute crop stage lengths calendar crop cycle length. function calculates duration different phenological stages based total crop cycle length, scaled reference 90-day cycle.

title: Get Crop Stages Lengths and Calendar — calculate_crop_stages
code: calculate_crop_stages(crop_cycle = 90)

title: Get Crop Stages Lengths and Calendar — calculate_crop_stages
text: crop_cycle Positive integer representing total length crop cycle days. Must whole number > 0. Default = 90 days.

title: Get Crop Stages Lengths and Calendar — calculate_crop_stages
text: named list six integer values (var_52 var_57) representing crop stage durations days. list can passed directly write_cro() via params argument create crop file custom phenology.

title: Get Crop Stages Lengths and Calendar — calculate_crop_stages
text: function calculates six crop stage variables based proportions reference 90-day crop cycle: var_52: Days sowing emergence (8/90 cycle = ~9%) var_53: Days sowing maximum canopy cover (100% cycle) var_54: Days sowing start canopy senescence (65/90 cycle = ~72%) var_55: Days sowing maturity (100% cycle) var_56: Days sowing start yield formation (52/90 cycle = ~58%) var_57: Duration yield formation period (20/90 cycle = ~22%) values rounded nearest integer day.

title: Get Crop Stages Lengths and Calendar — calculate_crop_stages
code: # Default 90-day cycle calculate_crop_stages() #> $var_52 #> [1] 8 #>  #> $var_53 #> [1] 90 #>  #> $var_54 #> [1] 65 #>  #> $var_55 #> [1] 90 #>  #> $var_56 #> [1] 52 #>  #> $var_57 #> [1] 20 #>  #> attr(,"description") #>                     var_52                     var_53  #>        "Days to emergence"   "Days to maximum canopy"  #>                     var_54                     var_55  #>       "Days to senescence"         "Days to maturity"  #>                     var_56                     var_57  #>  "Days to yield formation" "Yield formation duration"   # 120-day maize cycle maize_stages <- calculate_crop_stages(crop_cycle = 120)  # Use with write_cro to create a crop file if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   stages <- calculate_crop_stages(crop_cycle = 130)   write_cro(     path = "crop/",     crop_name = "maize-130d",     params = stages   ) } # }

title: Calculate Harvest Index Building Parameters — calculate_harvest_index_timing
code: calculate_harvest_index_timing(flowering_date = 35)

title: Calculate Harvest Index Building Parameters — calculate_harvest_index_timing
text: flowering_date Integer. Number days sowing/planting flowering. Must positive. Default = 35 days

title: Calculate Harvest Index Building Parameters — calculate_harvest_index_timing
text: Named list two elements: var_60 Days start HI building var_64 Days start linear HI increase

title: Calculate Harvest Index Building Parameters — calculate_harvest_index_timing
text: function calculates two key AquaCrop parameters: var_60: Start harvest index building (days sowing) Set equal flowering date var_64: Start linear harvest index increase (days sowing) Set 5 days flowering (flowering_date - 5) harvest index typically starts building around flowering increases linearly grain filling maturity.

title: Calculate Harvest Index Building Parameters — calculate_harvest_index_timing
code: # Default maize flowering (35 days) calculate_harvest_index_timing(35) #> $var_60 #> [1] 35 #>  #> $var_64 #> [1] 30 #>  # Returns: list(var_60 = 35, var_64 = 30)  # Wheat flowering (90 days) calculate_harvest_index_timing(90) #> $var_60 #> [1] 90 #>  #> $var_64 #> [1] 85 #>  # Returns: list(var_60 = 90, var_64 = 85)

title: Calculate Plant Population Density — calculate_plant_density
text: Calculate number plants per hectare based plant spacing row spacing. useful determining planting density AquaCrop simulations field planning.

title: Calculate Plant Population Density — calculate_plant_density
code: calculate_plant_density(plant_spacing = 0.3, row_spacing = 0.8)  plant_density(plant_spacing = 0.3, row_spacing = 0.8)

title: Calculate Plant Population Density — calculate_plant_density
text: plant_spacing Numeric. Distance plants within row (meters). Must positive (> 0). Default = 0.3 m row_spacing Numeric. Distance rows (meters). Must positive (> 0). Default = 0.8 m

title: Calculate Plant Population Density — calculate_plant_density
text: Integer representing number plants per hectare (plants/ha)

title: Calculate Plant Population Density — calculate_plant_density
text: calculation uses formula: $$plants/ha = \frac{10000}{plant\_spacing \times row\_spacing}$$ 10,000 m² = 1 hectare. result rounded (ceiling) ensure least calculated number plants per hectare.

title: Calculate Plant Population Density — calculate_plant_density
text: Maize: Low density: 30,000-40,000 plants/ha (0.75m × 0.40m) Medium density: 50,000-70,000 plants/ha (0.75m × 0.20m) High density: 80,000-100,000 plants/ha (0.50m × 0.20m) Wheat: Typical: 250-400 plants/m² = 2,500,000-4,000,000 plants/ha Soybean: Low: 250,000-350,000 plants/ha (0.75m × 0.05m) High: 400,000-500,000 plants/ha (0.50m × 0.05m)

title: Calculate Plant Population Density — calculate_plant_density
code: # Default maize spacing (0.3m × 0.8m) calculate_plant_density() #> [1] 41667 # Returns: 41,667 plants/ha  # Using alias plant_density(plant_spacing = 0.40, row_spacing = 0.75) #> [1] 33334 # Returns: 33,334 plants/ha  # Dense maize planting calculate_plant_density(plant_spacing = 0.20, row_spacing = 0.60) #> [1] 83334 # Returns: 83,334 plants/ha  # Cotton spacing plant_density(0.15, 1.0) #> [1] 66667 # Returns: 66,667 plants/ha  # Calculate for multiple scenarios spacings <- data.frame(   plant_spacing = c(0.20, 0.30, 0.40),   row_spacing = c(0.75, 0.75, 0.75) ) spacings$plants_per_ha <- mapply(   calculate_plant_density,   spacings$plant_spacing,   spacings$row_spacing )

title: Initialize AquaCrop Project Structure — init_aquacrop
text: path Character string specifying root directory project created. Default current working directory ("."). copy_exe Logical; TRUE (default), copies AquaCrop executable package project directory. copy_docs Logical; TRUE (default), copies LICENSE AUTHORS.md files package. copy_templates Logical; TRUE (default), copies template simulation files SIMUL/ directory. overwrite Logical; TRUE, overwrites existing directory structure. Default: FALSE.

title: Initialize AquaCrop Project Structure — init_aquacrop
text: Invisibly returns normalized path created directory.

title: Initialize AquaCrop Project Structure — init_aquacrop
text: function creates following directory structure: CLIMATE/ - Climate input files (temperature, rainfall, ETo) CROP/ - Crop parameter files LIST/ - Project simulation list files MANAGEMENT/ - Field management practice files OUTP/ - Simulation output files PARAM/ - General parameter files SIMUL/ - Simulation configuration files SOIL/ - Soil profile characteristic files appropriate AquaCrop executable operating system (Windows, macOS, Linux) automatically copied package installation. Template simulation files copied SIMUL/ directory: AggregationResults.SIM - Aggregation output configuration DailyResultsFullList.SIM - Daily results output configuration MaunaLoa.CO2 - CO2 concentration data ParticularResultsFullList.SIM - Particular results output configuration

title: AquaCrop Management Parameters — ManData
text: default values represent baseline management scenario : mulching (var_03 = 0%) Moderate soil fertility stress (var_05 = 42%) soil bunds (var_06 = 0 m) Moderate weed pressure (var_09 = 25%) multiple cuttings (var_13 = 0) special harvest timing (var_20 = 0)

title: Get access to CO2 Files Bundled in aquacroptools — path_to_file

title: Get access to CO2 Files Bundled in aquacroptools — path_to_file

title: Get access to CO2 Files Bundled in aquacroptools — path_to_file
text: path = NULL: character vector listing available CO2 files path specified: full file system path requested CO2 file

title: Get access to CO2 Files Bundled in aquacroptools — path_to_file
text: Historical Observations: MaunaLoa.CO2: Atmospheric CO2 Mauna Loa Observatory (observed data) GlobalAverage.CO2: Global average atmospheric CO2 concentrations IPCC Scenarios: IPCC-BERN_A1B.CO2: IPCC BERN model A1B scenario RCP Scenarios (Representative Concentration Pathways - used IPCC AR5): RCP2-6.CO2: RCP 2.6 - Strong mitigation (radiative forcing peaks ~3 W/m² declines) RCP4-5.CO2: RCP 4.5 - Moderate mitigation (stabilizes ~4.5 W/m²) RCP6-0.CO2: RCP 6.0 - Intermediate pathway (stabilizes ~6 W/m²) RCP8-5.CO2: RCP 8.5 - High emissions (rises ~8.5 W/m² 2100) SSP Scenarios (Shared Socioeconomic Pathways - used IPCC AR6): SSP1_1.9.CO2: SSP1-1.9 - low emissions (~1.9 W/m²) SSP1_2.6.CO2: SSP1-2.6 - Low emissions (~2.6 W/m²) SSP2_4.5.CO2: SSP2-4.5 - Moderate emissions (~4.5 W/m²) SSP3_7.0.CO2: SSP3-7.0 - High emissions (~7.0 W/m²) SSP5_8.5.CO2: SSP5-8.5 - high emissions (~8.5 W/m²)

title: Get access to CO2 Files Bundled in aquacroptools — path_to_file
text: typically need call function directly. write_cli function automatically selects copies appropriate CO2 file based scenario parameter. However, function useful : Listing available CO2 scenarios Reading CO2 data analysis Manually copying CO2 files custom locations function uses system.file() locate files within installed package, ensuring paths work across different operating systems installation locations.

title: Get access to CO2 Files Bundled in aquacroptools — path_to_file
code: # List all available CO2 files path_to_file() #>  [1] "AUTHORS.md"                    "AggregationResults.SIM"        #>  [3] "DailyResultsFullList.SIM"      "GlobalAverage.CO2"             #>  [5] "IPCC-BERN_A1B.CO2"             "LICENSE"                       #>  [7] "MaunaLoa.CO2"                  "ParticularResultsFullList.SIM" #>  [9] "RCP2-6.CO2"                    "RCP4-5.CO2"                    #> [11] "RCP6-0.CO2"                    "RCP8-5.CO2"                    #> [13] "SSP1_1.9.CO2"                  "SSP1_2.6.CO2"                  #> [15] "SSP2_4.5.CO2"                  "SSP3_7.0.CO2"                  #> [17] "SSP5_8.5.CO2"                  "aquacrop.exe"                  #> [19] "aquacrop_linux"                "aquacrop_macos"                 # Get path to historical Mauna Loa data (used by default in write_cli) co2_hist <- path_to_file("MaunaLoa.CO2")  # Get paths to climate scenario files co2_rcp45 <- path_to_file("RCP4-5.CO2") co2_ssp126 <- path_to_file("SSP1_2.6.CO2")  # Read CO2 data for analysis if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   # Read historical CO2 concentrations   hist_data <- readLines(path_to_file("MaunaLoa.CO2"))    # Compare different scenarios   rcp26_data <- readLines(path_to_file("RCP2-6.CO2"))   rcp85_data <- readLines(path_to_file("RCP8-5.CO2"))    # Note: Typically you use write_cli() instead, which handles this automatically:   write_cli(stn = "Wakanda", scenario = "rcp45")  # Automatically uses RCP4-5.CO2   write_cli(stn = "Wakanda", scenario = "hist")   # Automatically uses MaunaLoa.CO2 } # }

title: Round to Nearest Multiple — round_to
text: Round numeric values nearest multiple specified number. Useful standardizing parameter values creating rounded intervals.

title: Round to Nearest Multiple — round_to
code: round_to(x, to = 5, threshold = to/2)

title: Round to Nearest Multiple — round_to
text: x Numeric vector round Numeric value specifying multiple round . Default = 5 threshold Numeric value specifying threshold rounding . Values x %% >= threshold round , otherwise round . Default = /2 (standard rounding)

title: Round to Nearest Multiple — round_to
text: Numeric vector length x values rounded nearest multiple .

title: Round to Nearest Multiple — round_to
text: function rounds values nearest multiple : remainder (x %% ) greater equal threshold, value rounds next multiple Otherwise, rounds previous multiple default, threshold = /2 provides standard rounding behavior (e.g., 47.5 rounds 50 = 5).

title: Round to Nearest Multiple — round_to
code: # Round to nearest 5 round_to(47, to = 5)   # Returns 45 #> [1] 45 round_to(48, to = 5)   # Returns 50 #> [1] 50 round_to(50, to = 5)   # Returns 50 #> [1] 50 round_to(52, to = 5)   # Returns 50 #> [1] 50  # Round to nearest 10 round_to(23, to = 10)  # Returns 20 #> [1] 20 round_to(27, to = 10)  # Returns 30 #> [1] 30 round_to(25, to = 10)  # Returns 30 (standard rounding) #> [1] 30  # Round to nearest integer round_to(47.8, to = 1) # Returns 48 #> [1] 48 round_to(47.3, to = 1) # Returns 47 #> [1] 47  # Round to nearest 0.5 round_to(3.2, to = 0.5)  # Returns 3.0 #> [1] 3 round_to(3.7, to = 0.5)  # Returns 3.5 #> [1] 3.5  # Works with vectors round_to(c(12, 17, 23, 28), to = 5)  # Returns c(10, 15, 25, 30) #> [1] 10 15 25 30  # Custom threshold (round up at 3 instead of 2.5 for to = 5) round_to(47, to = 5, threshold = 3)  # Returns 45 #> [1] 45 round_to(48, to = 5, threshold = 3)  # Returns 50 #> [1] 50

title: Write AquaCrop Climate File — write_cli
text: Write AquaCrop v7.0 (August 2022) climate (.CLI) file references temperature, ETo, rainfall files, along appropriate CO2 concentration file simulation scenario.

title: Write AquaCrop Climate File — write_cli
code: write_cli(   path = "weather/",   stn = "station",   eol = "windows",   scenario = "hist",   check_files = TRUE )

title: Write AquaCrop Climate File — write_cli
text: path Directory path climate files located .CLI written. Default = "weather/" stn Station name identifier. .Tnx, .ETo, .PLU files station name must exist path. Default = "station" eol End--line character style output file. Options: "windows", "unix", "linux", "macOS". Default = "windows" scenario CO2 scenario use. Options: "hist" (historical/Mauna Loa), "rcp26", "rcp45", "rcp60", "rcp85", "ssp119", "ssp126", "ssp245", "ssp370", "ssp585". Default = "hist" check_files Logical. TRUE, checks three required climate files (.Tnx, .ETo, .PLU) exist writing .CLI file. Default = TRUE

title: Write AquaCrop Climate File — write_cli
text: Invisibly returns full path created .CLI file. function also copies appropriate CO2 file specified path already exist .

title: Write AquaCrop Climate File — write_cli
text: .CLI file main climate file references: Temperature file (.Tnx) Reference evapotranspiration file (.ETo) Rainfall file (.PLU) CO2 concentration file (.CO2) Important: default, function validates three climate files (.Tnx, .ETo, .PLU) exist creating .CLI file. Set check_files = FALSE skip validation (recommended unless sure files created later).

title: Write AquaCrop Climate File — write_cli
text: hist: Historical data Mauna Loa Observatory (MaunaLoa.CO2) RCP scenarios: Representative Concentration Pathways rcp26: RCP 2.6 (low emissions) rcp45: RCP 4.5 (intermediate emissions) rcp60: RCP 6.0 (intermediate-high emissions) rcp85: RCP 8.5 (high emissions) SSP scenarios: Shared Socioeconomic Pathways ssp119: SSP1-1.9 (low emissions) ssp126: SSP1-2.6 (low emissions) ssp245: SSP2-4.5 (intermediate emissions) ssp370: SSP3-7.0 (high emissions) ssp585: SSP5-8.5 (high emissions)

title: Write AquaCrop Climate File — write_cli
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   # First, create the required climate files   data("weather")   write_plu(data = weather, stn = "Wakanda", path = "weather/")   write_eto(data = weather, stn = "Wakanda", path = "weather/")   write_tnx(data = weather, stn = "Wakanda", path = "weather/")    # Then write CLI file with historical CO2   write_cli(     path = "weather/",     stn = "Wakanda",     scenario = "hist"   )    # Write CLI file with RCP 4.5 scenario   write_cli(     path = "weather/",     stn = "Wakanda",     scenario = "rcp45"   )    # Skip validation (not recommended)   write_cli(     path = "weather/",     stn = "Wakanda",     scenario = "hist",     check_files = FALSE   ) } # }

what: Write Complete AquaCrop Climate Dataset — write_climate
title: Write Complete AquaCrop Climate Dataset — write_climate
text: Convenience wrapper function writes required AquaCrop climate files (.PLU, .ETo, .Tnx, .CLI) single call. ensures files created consistently station name settings.

title: Write Complete AquaCrop Climate Dataset — write_climate
code: write_climate(   data = NULL,   path = "weather/",   stn = NULL,   var_name_rain = "rain",   var_name_et0 = "et0",   var_name_tmin = "tmin",   var_name_tmax = "tmax",   scenario = "hist",   eol = "windows",   syear = NULL,   eyear = NULL )

title: Write Complete AquaCrop Climate Dataset — write_climate
text: data Data frame containing complete weather data columns: year, month, day (date information) rain var_name_rain (rainfall, mm) et0 var_name_et0 (reference evapotranspiration, mm/day) tmin var_name_tmin (minimum temperature,  deg C) tmax var_name_tmax (maximum temperature, deg C) station (optional, station name) path Directory path climate files written. Default = "weather/" (relative current working directory). See write_plu, write_eto, write_tnx, write_cli details. stn Station name identifier. Default = NULL (extracted data available, otherwise uses "station"). See write_plu, write_eto, write_tnx, write_cli details. var_name_rain Name rainfall column data. Default = "rain". Passed write_plu var_name. var_name_et0 Name ETo column data. Default = "et0". Passed write_eto var_name. var_name_tmin Name minimum temperature column data. Default = "tmin". Passed write_tnx var_name_min. var_name_tmax Name maximum temperature column data. Default = "tmax". Passed write_tnx var_name_max. scenario CO2 scenario .CLI file. Options: "hist" (historical/Mauna Loa), "rcp26", "rcp45", "rcp60", "rcp85", "ssp119", "ssp126", "ssp245", "ssp370", "ssp585". Default = "hist". See write_cli details scenarios. eol End--line character style output files. Options: "windows", "unix", "linux", "macOS". Default = "windows". See write_plu, write_eto, write_tnx, write_cli details. syear Start year data period (extracted data provided). Default = NULL. See write_plu, write_eto, write_tnx details. eyear End year data period (extracted data provided). Default = NULL. See write_plu, write_eto, write_tnx details.

title: Write Complete AquaCrop Climate Dataset — write_climate
text: Invisibly returns named list paths created files: cli Path .CLI file (write_cli) plu Path .PLU file (write_plu) eto Path .ETo file (write_eto) tnx Path .Tnx file (write_tnx)

title: Write Complete AquaCrop Climate Dataset — write_climate
text: function creates complete set AquaCrop climate files correct order: Creates output directory exist Writes .PLU file (rainfall data) using write_plu Writes .ETo file (reference evapotranspiration data) using write_eto Writes .Tnx file (temperature data) using write_tnx Writes .CLI file (climate file references files) using write_cli Copies appropriate CO2 file based scenario files use station name, ensuring consistency. function passes appropriate parameters underlying write function.

title: Write Complete AquaCrop Climate Dataset — write_climate
text: Arguments passed underlying functions follows: data passed write_plu, write_eto, write_tnx path passed write functions stn passed write functions var_name_rain passed write_plu var_name var_name_et0 passed write_eto var_name var_name_tmin passed write_tnx var_name_min var_name_tmax passed write_tnx var_name_max scenario passed write_cli eol passed write functions syear, eyear passed write_plu, write_eto, write_tnx

title: Write Complete AquaCrop Climate Dataset — write_climate
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   # Load example weather data   data("weather")    # Write all climate files with defaults   files <- write_climate(     data = weather,     path = "weather/",     stn = "Wakanda"   )    # Access individual file paths   files$cli   files$plu    # Write climate files with RCP 4.5 scenario   write_climate(     data = weather,     path = "weather/",     stn = "Wakanda_Station",     scenario = "rcp45"   )    # Write climate files with custom column names   write_climate(     data = my_weather_data,     path = "climate/",     stn = "MyStation",     var_name_rain = "precipitation",     var_name_et0 = "pet",     var_name_tmin = "temp_min",     var_name_tmax = "temp_max"   )    # Using data with station column (backward compatibility)   write_climate(data = weather_with_station, path = "climate/")    # Specify year range explicitly   write_climate(     data = weather,     path = "weather/",     stn = "Wakanda",     syear = 2000,     eyear = 2010   ) } # }

title: Write AquaCrop Crop File — write_cro
text: Write AquaCrop v7.0 (August 2022) crop (.CRO) file containing crop-specific parameters. file specifies physiological characteristics, growth parameters, water stress responses, crop-specific properties used AquaCrop simulation.

title: Write AquaCrop Crop File — write_cro
code: write_cro(   path = "crop/",   crop_name = "generic-crop-name",   eol = "windows",   params = NULL )

title: Write AquaCrop Crop File — write_cro
text: path Directory path output .CRO file written. Default = "crop/" crop_name Name identifier crop (used output filename). Default = "generic-crop-name" eol End--line character style output file. Options: "windows", "linux", "unix", "macOS". Default = "windows" params Named list crop parameter values override defaults CropData. Parameter names var_02 var_84 (e.g., list(var_02 = 7.0, var_35 = 1.05)). Unspecified parameters use default values CropData.

title: Write AquaCrop Crop File — write_cro
text: Invisibly returns full path created .CRO file. Creates colon-delimited .CRO file specified directory format: <<crop_name>>.CRO containing crop parameters descriptions required AquaCrop v7.0.

title: Write AquaCrop Crop File — write_cro
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   # Write default crop file   write_cro(path = "crops/", crop_name = "maize")    # Write custom crop with modified parameters   write_cro(     path = "crops/",     crop_name = "maize-irrigated",     params = list(       var_35 = 1.10,  # Higher crop coefficient       var_50 = 0.85,  # Max canopy cover       var_64 = 35     # Reference harvest index     )   )    # Write crop with phenology parameters   write_cro(     path = "crops/",     crop_name = "wheat",     params = list(       var_52 = 10,    # Days to emergence       var_53 = 120,   # Days to max rooting       var_54 = 100,   # Days to senescence       var_55 = 130    # Days to maturity     )   ) } # }

what: Write AquaCrop Reference Evapotranspiration File — write_eto

text: Write AquaCrop v7.0 (August 2022) reference evapotranspiration (.ETo) file containing daily ETo data use crop simulations.

title: Write AquaCrop Reference Evapotranspiration File — write_eto
code: write_eto(   path = "weather/",   stn = NULL,   data = NULL,   var_name = "et0",   syear = NULL,   eyear = NULL,   eol = "windows",   record_type = 1,   first_day = 1,   first_month = 1 )  write_et0(   path = "weather/",   stn = NULL,   data = NULL,   var_name = "et0",   syear = NULL,   eyear = NULL,   eol = "windows",   record_type = 1,   first_day = 1,   first_month = 1 )

title: Write AquaCrop Reference Evapotranspiration File — write_eto
text: path Directory path output .ETo file written. Default = "weather/" stn Station name identifier weather station. Default = NULL (extracted data available) data Data frame containing daily ETo data. Can either: Columns: station, year, month, day, et0 (legacy format), Columns: year, month, day, et0 (new format) var_name Name ETo column data frame. Default = "et0" syear Start year data period (extracted data provided). Default = NULL eyear End year data period (extracted data provided). Default = NULL eol End--line character style output file. Options: "windows", "unix", "linux", "macOS". Default = "windows" record_type Type temporal aggregation. Options: 1 = daily, 2 = 10-daily, 3 = monthly. Default = 1 first_day First day record. 10-daily: 1, 11, 21; monthly: 1. Default = 1 first_month First month record (1-12). Default = 1

title: Write AquaCrop Reference Evapotranspiration File — write_eto

title: Write AquaCrop Reference Evapotranspiration File — write_eto

title: Write Fixed-Width Format File — write_fwf
text: Write data fixed-width format (FWF) file column specified character width. function creates properly aligned text files commonly used data exchange legacy systems specific file format requirements like AquaCrop climate files.

title: Write Fixed-Width Format File — write_fwf
code: write_fwf(   x,   file,   width,   justify = "l",   replace_na = "NA",   eol = "windows",   append = TRUE )

title: Write Fixed-Width Format File — write_fwf
text: x Data frame write file. columns converted character format. Factor columns automatically converted character writing. file Character string specifying output file path. Can relative absolute path. width Numeric value vector specifying character width column: Single value: columns use width (e.g., width = 10) Vector: column uses corresponding width (e.g., width = c(10, 15, 8)) single value provided multi-column data frame, recycled columns. justify Character string specifying text alignment within column width: "l": Left-align columns "r": Right-align columns Multi-character string: Align column individually (e.g., "lrl" left, right, left alignment three columns) Default = "l". single character provided multi-column data frame, recycled columns. replace_na Character string use place NA values. Default = "NA" eol End--line character style output file. Options: "windows": Windows-style line endings (\r\n) "unix", "linux", "macOS": Unix-style line endings (\n) Default = "windows" append Logical. TRUE, append existing file. FALSE, overwrite existing file. Default = TRUE

title: Write Fixed-Width Format File — write_fwf
text: Invisibly returns NULL. function called side effect writing data file.

title: Write Fixed-Width Format File — write_fwf
text: function provides lightweight solution writing fixed-width format files without additional dependencies beyond base R readr. particularly useful : Creating AquaCrop climate data files (.PLU, .ETo, .Tnx) Generating data files legacy systems Producing human-readable aligned text output

title: Write Fixed-Width Format File — write_fwf
text: Text aligned within specified width using sprintf formatting: Left-aligned ("l"): Text starts left edge, padded right Right-aligned ("r"): Text ends right edge, padded left

title: Write Fixed-Width Format File — write_fwf
text: Factor columns automatically converted character NA values replaced string specified replace_na data formatted character strings specified widths

title: Write Fixed-Width Format File — write_fwf
text: default, data appended existing files (append = TRUE) Use append = FALSE overwrite existing files Column names included output

title: Write Fixed-Width Format File — write_fwf
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   # Create sample data   df <- data.frame(     year = c(2020, 2021, 2022),     rainfall = c(850.5, 920.3, 780.1),     temp = c(25.2, 26.1, 24.8)   )    # Write with uniform width, left-aligned   write_fwf(     x = df,     file = "output.txt",     width = 10,     justify = "l",     append = FALSE   )    # Write with different widths per column, right-aligned   write_fwf(     x = df,     file = "output.txt",     width = c(6, 10, 10),     justify = "r",     append = FALSE   )    # Write with mixed alignment (left, right, right)   write_fwf(     x = df,     file = "output.txt",     width = c(6, 10, 10),     justify = "lrr",     append = FALSE   )    # Append to existing file   write_fwf(     x = df[1:2, ],     file = "output.txt",     width = 10,     justify = "r",     append = TRUE   )    # Handle NA values with custom replacement   df_na <- data.frame(     year = c(2020, 2021, NA),     value = c(100, NA, 150)   )    write_fwf(     x = df_na,     file = "output.txt",     width = 10,     justify = "r",     replace_na = "-9999",     append = FALSE   )    # Unix-style line endings   write_fwf(     x = df,     file = "output.txt",     width = 10,     justify = "r",     eol = "unix",     append = FALSE   ) } # }

title: Write AquaCrop Management File — write_man
text: Write AquaCrop v7.0 (August 2022) management (.MAN) file containing field crop management parameters. file specifies management practices mulch cover, soil fertility, weeds, harvesting practices applied AquaCrop simulation.

title: Write AquaCrop Management File — write_man
code: write_man(   path = "man/",   management_name = "generic-management",   eol = "windows",   params = list() )

title: Write AquaCrop Management File — write_man
text: path Directory path output .MAN file written. Default = "man/" management_name Name identifier management scenario (used output filename). Default = "generic-management" eol End--line character style output file. Options: "windows", "linux", "unix", "macOS". Default = "windows" params Named list management parameter values override defaults ManData. Parameter names var_02 var_21 (e.g., list(var_02 = 7.0, var_04 = 50)). Unspecified parameters use default values ManData. See details complete parameter list valid ranges.

title: Write AquaCrop Management File — write_man
text: Invisibly returns full path created .MAN file. Creates colon-delimited .MAN file specified directory format: <<management_name>>.MAN containing management parameters descriptions required AquaCrop v7.0.

title: Write AquaCrop Management File — write_man
text: ManData contains 20 management parameters (var_02 var_21) following valid ranges: var_02: AquaCrop version (typically 7.0) var_03: Ground surface covered mulches (0-100 %) var_04: Mulch effect soil evaporation reduction (0-100 %) var_05: Soil fertility stress degree (0-100 %) var_06: Height soil bunds (0+ m) var_07: Surface runoff affected practices (0-100 %) var_08: Surface runoff completely prevented (0-100 %) var_09: Relative weed cover canopy closure (0-100 %) var_10: Increase relative weed cover mid-season (0+ %) var_11: Shape factor canopy cover expansion weed field var_12: Replacement weeds self-thinned cover (0-100 %) var_13: Multiple cuttings flag (0 = considered, 1 = considered) var_14: Canopy cover cutting (0-100 %) var_15: Increase Canopy Growth Coefficient cutting (0+ %) var_16: First day cutting window (1+ days) var_17: Number days cutting window (-9 = total cycle) var_18: Timing multiple cuttings var_19: Time criterion cuttings var_20: Final harvest maturity flag (0 = considered, 1 = considered) var_21: Start growing cycle cuttings list (1+ -9)

title: Write AquaCrop Management File — write_man
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   # Write default management file   write_man(path = "management/", management_name = "default-management")    # Write custom management with modified parameters   write_man(     path = "management/",     management_name = "irrigated-maize",     params = list(var_03 = 20, var_04 = 60, var_05 = 30)   ) } # }

what: Write AquaCrop Soil Profile File — write_sol
title: Write AquaCrop Soil Profile File — write_sol
text: Write AquaCrop v7.0 (August 2022) soil profile (.SOL) file containing soil hydraulic properties characteristics soil horizon. file defines soil water retention, hydraulic conductivity, physical properties used water balance calculations.

title: Write AquaCrop Soil Profile File — write_sol
code: write_sol(   path = "soil/",   site_name = "default-site",   cn = 46,   rew = 5,   texture = c("loam", "silt loam"),   thickness = c(0.5, 1),   eol = "windows" )

title: Write AquaCrop Soil Profile File — write_sol
text: path Directory path .SOL file written. Default = "soil/" site_name Name identifier soil profile (used filename). Default = "default-site" cn Numeric. Soil curve number runoff estimation (0-100). Default = 46 CN < 60: Low runoff potential (permeable soils, high infiltration) CN 60-75: Moderate runoff potential CN 75-85: High runoff potential CN > 85: high runoff potential (impermeable surfaces) rew Numeric. Readily evaporable water top layer (mm). Default = 5 Typical range: 4-12 mm depending soil texture texture Character vector soil texture names horizon. Must USDA classification. See SoilWater valid textures. Default = c("loam", "silt loam") thickness Numeric vector soil horizon thickness meters. Must length texture. Default = c(0.5, 1.0) eol End--line character style output file. Options: "windows", "unix", "linux", "macOS". Default = "windows"

title: Write AquaCrop Soil Profile File — write_sol
text: Invisibly returns full path created .SOL file.

title: Write AquaCrop Soil Profile File — write_sol
text: .SOL file contains soil profile information organized horizons (layers). horizon, following properties specified SoilWater dataset:

title: Write AquaCrop Soil Profile File — write_sol
text: SAT: Saturation point (vol%, 0 kPa) FC: Field capacity (vol%, -33 kPa) WP: Wilting point (vol%, -1500 kPa) Ksat: Saturated hydraulic conductivity (mm/day)

title: Write AquaCrop Soil Profile File — write_sol
text: Penetrability: Root zone expansion rate (%, typically 100) Gravel: Volumetric gravel content (%) CRa, CRb: Capillary rise coefficients (dimensionless)

title: Write AquaCrop Soil Profile File — write_sol
text: number soil horizons automatically derived length texture thickness vectors. must length.

title: Write AquaCrop Soil Profile File — write_sol
text: CN value affects surface runoff generation. Choose based : Soil texture (clay soils higher CN) Soil structure infiltration capacity Surface conditions vegetation cover

title: Write AquaCrop Soil Profile File — write_sol
text: function writes soil profile characteristics (.SOL file). specify initial water content conditions, use write_swo create corresponding .SW0 file separately.

title: Write AquaCrop Soil Profile File — write_sol
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   # Simple: Single loam horizon   write_sol(     path = "soil/",     site_name = "field-1",     texture = "loam",     thickness = 1.0   )    # Two horizons with different textures   write_sol(     path = "soil/",     site_name = "layered-soil",     cn = 65,     rew = 7,     texture = c("sandy loam", "clay loam"),     thickness = c(0.4, 0.6)   )    # Three horizons   write_sol(     path = "soil/",     site_name = "deep-profile",     cn = 72,     rew = 6,     texture = c("loam", "clay loam", "clay"),     thickness = c(0.3, 0.5, 0.7)   )    # Complete workflow: SOL + SW0   write_sol(     site_name = "wakanda",     texture = c("loam", "clay"),     thickness = c(0.5, 1.0)   )    write_swo(     soil_name = "wakanda",     texture = c("loam", "clay"),     layer_thickness = c(0.5, 1.0),     initial_water = "FC"   ) } # }

what: Write AquaCrop Soil Profile File (.SOL) — write_sol2
title: Write AquaCrop Soil Profile File (.SOL) — write_sol2
text: Writes AquaCrop v7.0 (August 2022) soil profile file (.SOL) containing hydraulic physical properties soil horizon. Optionally, write_sw0 = TRUE, also writes matching initial soil-water content file (.SW0) calling write_swo().

title: Write AquaCrop Soil Profile File (.SOL) — write_sol2
code: write_sol2(   path = "soil/",   site_name = "default-site",   cn = 46,   rew = 5,   texture = c("loam", "silt loam"),   thickness = c(0.5, 1),   eol = "windows",   write_sw0 = TRUE,   initial_water = "WP",   salinity = "none",   initial_cc = -9,   initial_biomass = 0,   initial_root_depth = -9,   bund_water = 0,   bund_ec = 0,   swo_layers = NULL )

title: Write AquaCrop Soil Profile File (.SOL) — write_sol2
text: path Directory .SOL file written. Default: "soil/". site_name Name soil profile (used filename). Default: "default-site". cn Curve number runoff estimation (0–100). Default: 46. rew Readily evaporable water (mm) top soil layer. Default: 5. texture Character vector soil texture names (USDA classes). Must match entries SoilWater dataset. thickness Numeric vector horizon thicknesses (m). Must length texture. eol End--line style. One "windows", "unix", "linux", "macOS". Default: "windows". write_sw0 Logical; TRUE, also write corresponding .SW0 file calling write_swo(). Default: TRUE. initial_water Character string numeric. Specifies initial water content layers. Options: "WP" "wilting_point": Sets wilting point based soil texture "FC" "field_capacity": Sets field capacity (1.5 × WP) "SAT" "saturation": Sets saturation (2.5 × WP) Numeric value: Sets layers specified value (0-100%) used layers parameter provided. Default = "WP" salinity Character string numeric specifying soil salinity level layers. Options: "none", "slight", "slight", "moderate", "strong", "strong", numeric ECe value (0-100 dS/m). used layers parameter provided. Default = "none" initial_cc Numeric. Initial canopy cover can reached without water stress (%). Use -9.00 automatic calculation AquaCrop. Default = -9.00 initial_biomass Numeric. Biomass produced simulation start (ton/ha). Default = 0.000 initial_root_depth Numeric. Initial effective rooting depth without water stress (m). Use -9.00 automatic calculation. Default = -9.00 bund_water Numeric. Water layer stored soil bunds present (mm). Default = 0.0 bund_ec Numeric. Electrical conductivity water bunds (dS/m). Default = 0.00 swo_layers Optional data.frame pass layers write_swo(). NULL (default), write_swo() construct layers texture thickness.

title: Write AquaCrop Soil Profile File (.SOL) — write_sol2
text: Invisibly returns path written .SOL file.

title: Write AquaCrop Soil Profile File (.SOL) — write_sol2
text: .SOL file defines hydraulic physical parameters soil horizon, including: SAT: Saturation (vol %) FC: Field capacity (vol %) WP: Wilting point (vol %) Ksat: Saturated hydraulic conductivity (mm day⁻¹) Penetrability, Gravel, CRa, CRb: Additional physical characteristics number horizons inferred lengths texture thickness.

title: Write AquaCrop Soil Profile File (.SOL) — write_sol2
text: write_sw0 = TRUE, write_swo() called using path, site_name (soil_name), texture, thickness (layer_thickness), eol. following arguments passed : initial_water, salinity, initial_cc, initial_biomass, initial_root_depth, bund_water, bund_ec, swo_layers (layers).

title: Write AquaCrop Soil Profile File (.SOL) — write_sol2
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Basic soil profile (single horizon) write_sol(   path = "soil/",   site_name = "field1",   texture = "loam",   thickness = 1.0 )  # Two horizons with automatic SW0 generation (field capacity) write_sol(   path = "soil/",   site_name = "layered",   cn = 65,   rew = 7,   texture = c("sandy loam", "clay loam"),   thickness = c(0.4, 0.6),   initial_water = "FC",   salinity = "none" )  # Write only the .SOL file (no SW0) write_sol(   site_name = "soil_only",   texture = "loam",   thickness = 1.0,   write_sw0 = FALSE ) } # }

what: Write AquaCrop Soil Initial Water Content File — write_swo
title: Write AquaCrop Soil Initial Water Content File — write_swo
text: Write AquaCrop v7.0 (August 2022) soil initial water content (.SW0) file specifies initial water content soil layer start simulation. file defines soil moisture status planting simulation start.

title: Write AquaCrop Soil Initial Water Content File — write_swo
code: write_swo(   path = "soil/",   soil_name = "default-soil-init",   texture = "loam",   eol = "windows",   initial_cc = -9,   initial_biomass = 0,   initial_root_depth = -9,   bund_water = 0,   bund_ec = 0,   initial_water = "WP",   salinity = "none",   layer_thickness = 1,   layers = NULL )

title: Write AquaCrop Soil Initial Water Content File — write_swo
text: path Directory path .SW0 file written. Default = "soil/" soil_name Name identifier soil initial conditions file (used filename). Default = "default-soil-init" texture Character vector soil texture names layer. Valid textures: "sand", "loamy sand", "sandy loam", "loam", "silt loam", "silt", "sandy clay loam", "clay loam", "silty clay loam", "sandy clay", "silty clay", "clay". Default = "loam" eol End--line character style output file. Options: "windows", "unix", "linux", "macOS". Default = "windows" initial_cc Numeric. Initial canopy cover can reached without water stress (%). Use -9.00 automatic calculation AquaCrop. Default = -9.00 initial_biomass Numeric. Biomass produced simulation start (ton/ha). Default = 0.000 initial_root_depth Numeric. Initial effective rooting depth without water stress (m). Use -9.00 automatic calculation. Default = -9.00 bund_water Numeric. Water layer stored soil bunds present (mm). Default = 0.0 bund_ec Numeric. Electrical conductivity water bunds (dS/m). Default = 0.00 initial_water Character string numeric. Specifies initial water content layers. Options: "WP" "wilting_point": Sets wilting point based soil texture "FC" "field_capacity": Sets field capacity (1.5 × WP) "SAT" "saturation": Sets saturation (2.5 × WP) Numeric value: Sets layers specified value (0-100%) used layers parameter provided. Default = "WP" salinity Character string numeric specifying soil salinity level layers. Options: "none", "slight", "slight", "moderate", "strong", "strong", numeric ECe value (0-100 dS/m). used layers parameter provided. Default = "none" layer_thickness Numeric vector specifying thickness soil layer meters. used layers parameter provided. Default = 1.0 (single 1m layer) layers Data frame soil layer information. provided, overrides initial_water. Must contain columns: thickness: Layer thickness meters (m) water_content: Volumetric water content (vol%) ece: Electrical conductivity (dS/m). Optional, defaults 0

title: Write AquaCrop Soil Initial Water Content File — write_swo
text: Invisibly returns full path created .SW0 file.

title: Write AquaCrop Soil Initial Water Content File — write_swo
text: .SW0 file defines initial soil water conditions layer start AquaCrop simulation. conditions significantly affect early crop growth development.

title: Write AquaCrop Soil Initial Water Content File — write_swo
text: Canopy cover: Set -9.00 let AquaCrop calculate, specify actual value (0-100%) Biomass: Usually 0 planting, can specify pre-existing biomass Root depth: Set -9.00 automatic, specify initial depth meters

what: Soil Textures and Default Wilting Points:
title: Write AquaCrop Soil Initial Water Content File — write_swo
text: function uses SWOData dataset contains wilting point values 12 standard USDA soil textures. See data(SWOData) details.

title: Write AquaCrop Soil Initial Water Content File — write_swo
text: Wilting Point (WP): Minimum water plant survival (texture-specific) Field Capacity (FC): WP × 1.5 (approximate) Saturation (SAT): WP × 2.5 (approximate)

title: Write AquaCrop Soil Initial Water Content File — write_swo
text: Use salinity_to_ece convert qualitative descriptions ECe values: "none": 0 dS/m (non-saline) "slight": 3 dS/m (slightly saline) "moderate": 6 dS/m (moderately saline) "strong": 12 dS/m (strongly saline) "strong": 20 dS/m (strongly saline)

title: Write AquaCrop Soil Initial Water Content File — write_swo
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   # Simple: Single layer at wilting point, no salinity   write_swo(     path = "soil/",     soil_name = "loam-wp",     texture = "loam",     initial_water = "WP"   )    # Single layer at field capacity   write_swo(     soil_name = "sandy-loam-fc",     texture = "sandy loam",     initial_water = "FC"   )    # With salinity   write_swo(     soil_name = "saline-soil",     texture = "clay loam",     initial_water = "WP",     salinity = "moderate"   )    # Multiple layers with different textures   write_swo(     soil_name = "layered-profile",     texture = c("sandy loam", "loam", "clay loam"),     layer_thickness = c(0.3, 0.4, 0.3),     initial_water = "FC",     salinity = "slight"   )    # Mid-season with existing crop   write_swo(     soil_name = "mid-season-maize",     texture = "loam",     initial_water = "FC",     initial_cc = 60,     initial_biomass = 3.5,     initial_root_depth = 0.7   )    # Custom numeric values   write_swo(     soil_name = "custom",     texture = "loam",     layer_thickness = c(0.5, 0.5),     initial_water = 28,     salinity = 4.5   )    # Manual layers (full control)   layers <- data.frame(     thickness = c(0.3, 0.4, 0.3),     water_content = c(20, 25, 30),     ece = c(0, 3, 6)   )    write_swo(     soil_name = "manual-layers",     texture = c("Sandy Loam", "Loam", "Clay Loam"),     layers = layers   ) } # }

title: Write AquaCrop Temperature File — write_tnx
text: Write AquaCrop v7.0 (August 2022) temperature (.Tnx) file containing daily minimum maximum temperature data use crop simulations.

title: Write AquaCrop Temperature File — write_tnx
code: write_tnx(   path = "weather/",   stn = NULL,   data = NULL,   var_name_min = "tmin",   var_name_max = "tmax",   syear = NULL,   eyear = NULL,   eol = "windows",   record_type = 1,   first_day = 1,   first_month = 1 )

title: Write AquaCrop Temperature File — write_tnx
text: path Directory path output .Tnx file written. Default = "weather/" stn Station name identifier weather station. Default = NULL (extracted data available) data Data frame containing daily temperature data. Can either: Columns: station, year, month, day, tmin, tmax (legacy format), Columns: year, month, day, tmin, tmax (new format) var_name_min Name minimum temperature column data frame. Default = "tmin" var_name_max Name maximum temperature column data frame. Default = "tmax" syear Start year data period (extracted data provided). Default = NULL eyear End year data period (extracted data provided). Default = NULL eol End--line character style output file. Options: "windows", "unix", "linux", "macOS". Default = "windows" record_type Type temporal aggregation. Options: 1 = daily, 2 = 10-daily, 3 = monthly. Default = 1 first_day First day record. 10-daily: 1, 11, 21; monthly: 1. Default = 1 first_month First month record (1-12). Default = 1

title: Write AquaCrop Temperature File — write_tnx
text: Invisibly returns full path created .Tnx file. Creates formatted .Tnx file specified directory format: <>.Tnx containing daily minimum maximum temperature data required AquaCrop v7.0.

title: Write AquaCrop Temperature File — write_tnx
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   # Load example weather data   data("weather")    # Write temperature file with default column names   write_tnx(     path = "weather/",     stn = "Wakanda_Station",     data = weather   )    # Legacy format (backward compatibility)   write_tnx(data = weather_with_station, path = "climate/") } # }
